lanes.label: Rust native CLI
lanes.description: Primary Codex execution path implemented in the Rust server through the local Codex CLI JSONL runtime.

lanes.label: gRPC SDK bridge
lanes.description: Planned fallback lane that lets Rust delegate to the official TypeScript SDK through a gRPC bridge when direct native integration is insufficient.

officialIntegration.notes: BirdCoder standardizes Codex on the official TypeScript SDK contract while keeping the Rust-native CLI JSONL authority lane as the production baseline.

lanes.label: stdio Agent SDK bridge
lanes.description: Primary standardized lane routes Rust server requests into the bundled TypeScript bridge that wraps the upstream Claude Code Agent SDK and records native session transcripts.

lanes.label: Claude CLI print bridge
lanes.description: Ready fallback lane where the same TypeScript bridge invokes the real Claude Code CLI in non-interactive print mode when the Agent SDK package is not installed.

lanes.label: Remote control HTTP
lanes.description: Fallback lane for deployments where Claude Code is exposed as an externally managed remote-control endpoint behind the BirdCoder gateway.

officialIntegration.notes: BirdCoder standardizes Claude Code on the official Agent SDK contract, with a real Claude CLI print fallback inside the bundled TypeScript stdio bridge when Rust authority delegates SDK execution.

lanes.label: stdio SDK bridge
lanes.description: Primary standardized lane uses the bundled TypeScript SDK bridge behind Rust so Gemini runtime differences stay isolated outside the product surface while native transcripts stay queryable.

lanes.label: OpenAPI HTTP proxy
lanes.description: Fallback lane for environments where a direct upstream Gemini-compatible HTTP endpoint can be proxied by the Rust server.

officialIntegration.notes: BirdCoder standardizes Gemini on the Gemini CLI SDK contract and treats the bundled TypeScript stdio bridge as the primary authority delegation lane.

lanes.label: Rust native OpenCode server bridge
lanes.description: Primary OpenCode lane runs inside the Rust server by attaching to an existing OpenCode server or spawning `opencode serve`, then normalizing the native HTTP session API.

officialIntegration.notes: BirdCoder standardizes OpenCode on the official JavaScript SDK contract while the Rust authority owns the OpenAPI-native server lane.
